notes: Purchased the Legends Edition.  Launches and runs fine on Ultra High Settings with minor artifacts but very playable. Better performance than F1 2018.
title: F1® 2019 Anniversary Edition

notes: dx11 mode, medium settings, laggy cutscenes, froze during race once
title: F1® 2019 Anniversary Edition

notes: Had to run in DX11 mode, but mostly worked just fine. Some minor graphical glitches (black squares over some human/character models) but nothing that gets in the way of regular gameplay.
title: F1® 2019 Anniversary Edition

notes: Just take "DirectX 11" choice (a steam window appears at launch), "DirectX 12" not work
title: F1® 2019 Anniversary Edition

notes:
Works great, no noticeable lag (unlike F1 2018) and Force Feedback/Rumble works (at least on a DS4 controller). Did a few laps of Spain, and worked great.

Very minor thing, when creating my driver, the character models were "blocky" around the edges (like AA turned off, but worse). None of this while sitting in the car though.
title: F1® 2019 Anniversary Edition

notes: contrary to my previous report, i get a lot of crashes and freezes, nvidia driver version doesn't seem to matter, the latest game patch helps but something is still funky
title: F1® 2019 Anniversary Edition

notes: Getting around 80FPS average on highest graphics settings.
title: F1® 2019 Anniversary Edition

notes:
Further to my previous report I have decided to downgrade this game to "Silver"

To be clear, the game does run fine. Minor "blocky" graphical issues on character models as noted before, but nothing that makes it unplayable as long as you are using DX11 mode.

However, the game will crash quite frequently during the loading screen. This seems to happen most when returning to the main menu, and sometimes the game won't start the first time and has to be killed and relaunched. It's playable, but a little patience is required.
title: F1® 2019 Anniversary Edition

notes: The games doesn't start, and I selected the D3d11 option... On the same machine other games like Witcher 3 or Wolfenstein 2 work perfectly.
title: F1® 2019 Anniversary Edition

notes: Crashes with "umip: F1_2019.exe[27818] ip:14cfe4667 sp:227728: SGDT instruction cannot be used by applications." in dmesg.
title: F1® 2019 Anniversary Edition

notes: Game starts fine, but doesnt register my G29 steering wheel while other games do
title: F1® 2019 Anniversary Edition

notes: Works perfectly in DX11 mode, tested 35+ hours in both career mode and multiplayer. G29 wheel is recognized, uses correct button prompts, and force-feedback works as well (it feels odd compared to native F12017, but apparently this year's F1 has a different FF feel). Voice recognition works well. On my hardware, 4k resolution runs at 60+ FPS at "high" settings, 1080p runs at 60+ FPS at "ultra high". Only drops below 60FPS in the main menu and when navigating garage displays. Some graphical glitches appear from time to time, but identical artefacts can be seen when running the game on Windows.
title: F1® 2019 Anniversary Edition

notes:
Game is perfectly playable with the following issues:
- Blocky character models borders 
- Chrashes some what frequently when returning to the main menu, and sometimes the game won't start the first time and has to be killed and relaunched.

What works:
- Playing with G920, zero problems
- Internet and online works
- Everything else works
title: F1® 2019 Anniversary Edition

notes: Runs without any tweaks with DirectX 11. Game sometimes crashes after career race is finished and also on night races frame rates drop significantly. While in main menu, there are some weird artifacts
title: F1® 2019 Anniversary Edition

notes:
Have carried out minor system upgrades so submitting further report. Now played 65+ Hours. I was getting occasional freezes and video memory errors. I have reduced settings from Ultra High to High and runs perfectly on Proton 4.11-2
As mentioned above there are blocks to characters such as selecting drivers but during normal game play this is minimal and does not detract from the racing experience. The Bahrain night race is phenomenal. The game occasionally fails to close when saving and exiting a race but this is intermittent. Since the release of Ubuntu 18.04.3 LTS stability has increased however I am uncertain as to if this would have any bearing. Performance is also exceptional on Ubuntu 19.04
Very playable all round and a significant improvement on F1 2018 on the same settings.
title: F1® 2019 Anniversary Edition

notes: Works fine OOB. Had to lower graphics from Ultra to High to get better performance, but it runs just like in Windows. Haven't detect anything weird or something that affects gameplay appart of some FPS drops during first lap, but that's matter of amdgpu using Xbox controller, what it's supposed to be patched on 4.11-5.
title: F1® 2019

notes: Career Mode full race weekend at Melbourne, solid 100+FPS 1080p at high settings, no stutters. Only thing is desktop icon does not show option to run in dx11 so it fails to launch, but launching from library play button and choosing dx11 no problem.
title: F1® 2019
